OSCAR and JuliaLang Reading Group
Lecture notesFAQ

Basics of Julia

1) Basic syntax and operations

Variables and data types

x = 10               # Integer
y = 3.14             # Floating-point number
name = "Julia"      # String
is_active = true    # Boolean

Basic operations

# Arithmetic
sum = x + y
difference = x - y 
product = x * y
quotient = x / y
# Comparison
is_equal = (x == y)
is_greater = (x > y)

2) Control flow

Conditional statements

if x > y
    println("x is greater than y")
elseif x < y
    println("x is less than y")
else
    println("x is equal to y")
end

Loops

# For loop
for i in 1:5
    println(i)
end 
# While loop
count = 1
while count <= 5
    println(count)
    count += 1
end

3) Functions

function greet(name)
    return "Hello, $name!"
end 
message = greet("Julia")
println(message)

4) Arrays and Dictionaries

Arrays

arr = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]
push!(arr, 6)          # Add element
first_element = arr[1] # Access element

Dictionaries

dict = Dict("name" => "Julia", "age" => 10)
dict["location"] = "Programming" # Add key-value pair
name = dict["name"]              # Access value

5) Packages

using Pkg
Pkg.add("ExamplePackage")  # Install a package
using ExamplePackage        # Load a package

6) REPL and Scripts

  • REPL (Read-Eval-Print Loop): Interactive environment to run Julia commands
  • Scripts: Save Julia code in .jl files and run them using: 
    julia script_name.jl

    7) Resources for further learning

  • Official Julia Documentation
  • Julia Academy
